title: Bionano opens 2026 OGM event with 9 blood cancer talks
text: Bionano Genomics hosted its 2026 OGM Symposium featuring 9 presentations focused on optical genome mapping applications in blood cancer diagnostics and hematologic malignancies. The event showcased advances in OGM clinical applications, featuring speakers from leading academic medical centers and diagnostic laboratories. Key topics included: OGM utility in T-ALL diagnosis, detection of structural variants in B-cell lymphomas, application in multiple myeloma diagnostics, emerging use in cell and gene therapy selection. Clinical evidence presentations demonstrated OGM's superiority in detecting complex structural rearrangements compared to conventional cytogenetics. The symposium reinforced Bionano's positioning as the leader in optical genome mapping technology with broad clinical adoption. Presentations highlighted potential for scaling to 10,000+ samples annually using Stratys Compute system. The event provided valuable insights into end-user perspectives on technology benefits, clinical workflow integration, and market opportunities.
